title: senecio kosterae
intro: Senecio kosterae, also known as the Koster's curse, is a toxic plant species native to New Zealand that belongs to the daisy family. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine, but its toxic properties make it hazardous for internal consumption. However, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and poultices, to treat skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory properties. In moderation, Senecio kosterae may also have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and potentially treating wounds, although caution and expert guidance are necessary due to its toxicity.
